On 16 July 1945, a test in the atomic bomb race. How the US and Britain beat Germany and Japan through spying and espionage. Narrated by Robert Powell.

programmes.show_name: London's East End Before the War
programmes.show_description: We explore London's East End in the years before the Second World War - a world of cobbled streets and thick fog where thousands of Londoners live in cramped, sooty lice-infected tenements.
The B-24 Liberator more than proved its worth over Europe and the Pacific by patterning the B17 to deliver a blow to the Axis powers in WWII; however, it wasn't simply a bomb delivery system. S2 E2

The second most powerful man in Nazi Germany stole billions worth of art and treasure; the team is on the trail of sunken gold and missing masterpieces looted from Holland. S4 E1: Goering's Looted Art
James Holland and Mike Simpson examine the preparations for the invasion across southern England before crossing the Channel to Normandy and challenging many D-Day myths. S1 E1: D-Day

programmes.show_name: The Nazis: A Warning From History
programmes.show_description: This episode provides insights into how the Nazis' reign of terror in Poland was characterised by huge population upheavals, chaos, petty squabbles and sheer bloodlust. Ep4: The Wild East
The Avro Vulcan and the Panavia Tornado were completed for their development after the conclusion of World War II. We take an in-depth look at the impressive and then new features of these planes.
The Allied fronts in both western and eastern Europe had become faster-moving by 1944 - a new form of aircraft was needed to deliver swift powerful attacks against German lines. Ep5: Fighter Bombers
...Were You? On the 1st of September, 2004, terrorists took 1,100 people, including 777 children, hostage in the Beslan School in Russia - the ensuing massacre saw over 300 people killed.
The war in the Pacific nears its violent end as a drastic tactic triggers the nuclear age - brave men share their thoughts behind the war's most tragic events. Ep12: Final Victory
Ex-Prisoners of War tell of their time in captivity. It is a story of stark contrasts, of everyday existence and clandestine diversions. And the terrors of forced labour. Ep2: Captivity
After Nazi Germany's invasion and capture of France, brave French citizens take it upon themselves to wage a guerrilla campaign against their occupiers. S1 E13: The French Resistance

Times are hard as the miners strike; Jack has money and is now engaged to Jessie, but Tom threatens to expose him when he sleeps with the widowed sister of a war comrade. S1 E3: Fish in Woolly Jumpers
Ray is given the task of minding a racing pigeon that is due to race from London to Bradford. S8 E7: A Bird in the Hand is Worth Two in Shepherd's Bush
Rigsby returns from a Spanish holiday to find Alan has let out the two spare rooms; he's happy Miss Jones has returned but less enthusiastic about a single mother and her baby. S3 E1: That's My Boy

Mr. Brown tells the class the story of how he was left in an orphanage when he was a baby. However, his mother and father might be a lot closer to him than he ever imagined. S1 E12: How's Your Father?
It's the run-up to the 1983 General Election, and pressure mounts on DCI Gene Hunt and his team as they investigate a series of politically-motivated arson attacks. S3 E3
Alf loses his job and comes home to find he may have been burgled. It's an unhappy birthday but a surprise is in store. S7 E6: Unemployment
With The Darts, Lindisfarne and Yellow Dog. The legendary BAFTA-winning music and comedy show from Thames TV (1978). All in the best possible taste!
A US Army sergeant is killed as he tries to stop an armed robbery, but all might not be as it seems when the man's girlfriend is involved in the gang that committed the crime. S3 E11: The Hi-Jackers

In 1933, sightings began of a creature in Loch Ness. This programme examines evidence and rare footage to explore an enduring mystery. Narrated by Robert Powell.

The East End is rocked by the Luftwaffe in 1940 as whole areas are reduced to rubble; it emerges victorious into a new world, but the Docks are closed and the old East End will vanish within 30 years.

The B-25, often called 'the forgotten bomber', the mighty North American twin delivered the first blow in the fight back against Japan, in one of the most daring and audacious raids of all time. S2 E3

In 1943, the Nazis looted $50 million in gold from the Jewish community in Djerba in Tunisia; the loot is tracked to the family of an escaped Nazi in South America. S4 E2: The Mystery of Rommel's Gold
The real fighting begins as James and Mike travel inland and analyse the Canadians' first encounters with the Waffen SS in a forgotten but vital battle. S1 E2: Attrition

programmes.show_name: The Nazis: A Warning From History
programmes.show_description: Documentary. This episode shows how the Nazi invasion of the Soviet Union in June 1941 was a crucial catalyst for the radicalisation of the Nazi policy against the Jews. Ep5: The Road To Treblinka
The development and operational history of the best and most famous fighter aircraft of World War II, some of which changed the course of the conflict and led to the beginnings of new aerial warfare.
At the outbreak of WWII, the only heavy four-engined bombers in the war were either Russian or American - soon the Short Stirling, the Avro Lancaster, and the Liberator were added. Ep6: Heavy Bombers
There are some news events that change the world. We look at the war in Afghanistan and the attempts to defeat the Taliban.
George Michael's slide into self-destruction led to his sudden death at 53. Now expert pathologist, Dr Michael Hunter, investigates what was going on in the singer's body that caused him to die.
After years of captivity, Allied POW's prepare for liberation. But In the bitter winter of January 1945, prisoners are ordered out at gunpoint. It is the beginning of the Long March. Ep3: Freedom
We begin the countdown of America's most notorious unsolved mysteries and crimes by exploring who killed Marilyn Monroe and ask if one of America's most infamous killers got away with murder. E1
When Jack visits Sir Manners - the father of his late officer - and gives him a present, Manners offers him a job; elsewhere, Jack saves Tom from a beating. S1 E4: Swords and Pick Handles
When reformed Ron the Burglar manages to open Arthur's lock-up, Arthur realises it's time for Daley Enterprises to strengthen its warehouse security. S8 E8: Him Indoors
Rigsby attempts to have himself cast as male lead in a play written by effeminate lodger Hilary when he discovers that Ruth is playing the role of the love interest. S3 E2: Stage Struck
The students prepare to take their exams at the end of the first term, but are they ready? S1 E13: The Examination
Gene and the team must protect an undercover policewoman investigating a drugs ring on their patch, and Alex continues to be haunted by the ghostly constable. S3 E4
